goal: Commit the current baseline, then implement the recommended control-mode semantic cleanup so open-loop and closed-loop commands are no longer mixed.
deliverable: A code change set plus governed artifacts that separates raw duty control from velocity closed-loop control in PC and ESP paths.
constraints: Preserve the existing closed-loop control core.
Avoid a full protocol redesign in one turn.
Keep backward compatibility where practical, but prefer semantic clarity over silent ambiguous behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Open-loop mode only consumes explicit raw left/right duty commands.
Closed-loop drive commands are no longer implicitly reused as open-loop commands.
PC and binary host protocols expose a distinct raw-duty command path.
product_acceptance_criteria: The system behavior in MODE_OPEN_LOOP becomes explicit and understandable.
The user can intentionally choose raw duty testing versus velocity control from host interfaces.
manual_spot_checks: In MODE_CLOSED_LOOP, DRIVE/CTRL should work and RAW/MOTOR should be rejected or clearly gated.
In MODE_OPEN_LOOP, RAW/MOTOR should work and DRIVE should be rejected or clearly gated.
Binary SET_MODE + SET_RAW should provide an explicit ESP/PC open-loop path.
completion_language_policy: Do not claim field behavior is perfect without hardware validation. Claim only semantic cleanup and static verification.
delivery_truth_contract: Only claim the protocol and control-path changes that are implemented in the current source.
non_goals: Redesigning arbitration for all command sources.
Removing every legacy alias in one step.
inferred_assumptions: A semantic cleanup can be delivered without changing the broader robot dynamics architecture.
The existing MOTOR command can be preserved as a compatibility alias of an explicit raw-duty path.
